name: Kwame Raoul
bio: Kwame Raoul is the incumbent Illinois Attorney General, first elected in 2018 and running for a third term. He is a former state senator who succeeded Barack Obama in his South Side Chicago district in 2004. As AG, Raoul has led multistate litigation against the Trump administration on immigration, environmental rollbacks, and consumer protections. He is unopposed in the Democratic primary and will face Republican Robert Fioretti in November.
prior_office: Illinois State Senator (2004–2018); Illinois Attorney General (2019–present)

jacket.note: Unopposed in Democratic primary. Succeeded Barack Obama's state senate seat. Active anti-Trump litigation record as AG.

key_votes.summary: Led coalition of state AGs suing the Trump administration over immigration enforcement actions affecting Illinois residents.
Negotiated opioid settlement funds for Illinois from pharmaceutical manufacturers and distributors.

policy_positions: Resist federal overreach and Trump administration actions harming Illinois residents
